title: Sample Digital Twin State — ILLUSTRATIVE SYNTHETIC DATA
DANGER_READ_THIS_FIRST: EVERY VALUE IN THIS FILE IS FABRICATED. It describes no real pond. It exists solely so the webpage developer can build and test the PondTwin 3D visualisation against correctly-shaped data. It must never appear as evidence of anything. The webpage must render the illustrative_synthetic badge on the entire twin view when fed this file.

geometry.note: A fabricated simple geometry for rendering. Not a real bathymetry.

state_layers.note: This block exists to test the twin's PROVENANCE RENDERING — the requirement that measured, interpolated, and stale data look visibly different. See digital-twin.json#/the_provenance_rendering_requirement.

measured.description: Fabricated measured points at the three stations. In the real twin these render solid and sharp.

interpolated.description: The volume BETWEEN stations. In the real twin this must render with a VISIBLE UNCERTAINTY CUE that grows with distance from a station. This block carries a fabricated per-point confidence so the developer can wire that rendering.
interpolated.the_critical_rendering_requirement: A smooth confident gradient here is the most persuasive lie the product can tell. Volume far from any station MUST look less certain. See digital-twin.json REQ-TWIN-003.

sample_grid_points.note: midway between stations — low confidence, render uncertain
far corner, no nearby station — render VERY uncertain, near-transparent

operator_supplied.description: Stock data the farmer typed in. Must be badged distinctly — it is testimony, not measurement.
operator_supplied.note: In the real twin, a confident biomass number sourced here launders a guess into a fact. Badge it.

simulated.status: EMPTY — the simulation layer does not exist yet
simulated.note: In v1, PondTwin has no simulation core. This block is null on purpose. See digital-twin.json#/twin_layers (State — simulated). When the simulation exists, its output goes here in a VISUALLY DISTINCT channel, never blended with measured.

illustrative_alert.message_example: Bottom water at cage 4 is anoxic (0.2 mg/L at 6 m, measured 12 min ago). Rain forecast tonight. If the column mixes, that water reaches your fish. Consider running aerators at cages 3-5 this evening.
illustrative_alert.note: This is the alert LANGUAGE STYLE from dashboard-spec.json — measured fact plus mechanism plus concrete action, no score. The content is fabricated; the FORMAT is the real specification.

for_the_webpage_developer.what_this_is_for: Building and testing the 3D twin view — geometry rendering, the vertical slice interaction, and critically the PROVENANCE RENDERING that makes measured, interpolated, operator-supplied, and stale data look different.
for_the_webpage_developer.the_non_negotiable: When this file feeds the twin, the whole view carries the illustrative_synthetic badge. And the interpolated layer must demonstrate the uncertainty-rendering requirement, because that requirement is the twin's core honesty feature and this synthetic data exists partly to let you build it.
for_the_webpage_developer.on_real_data: Delete this file at repository v2.0.0 and remove synthetic badges in the same change.
